sections.title: The Problem
sections.promptHint: Make the investor feel the pain before introducing the solution. Include: who suffers, what they do today as a workaround, and the cost of the status quo in time or money. Use a specific, relatable scenario.
sections.ragQuery: problem pain point challenge customer frustration status quo

sections.title: The Solution
sections.promptHint: Describe the product in plain language. Lead with the 'aha moment' — the single insight that makes this different. Include: what it does, how it works at a high level, and the key differentiator. One sentence a non-technical investor can repeat.
sections.ragQuery: solution product features how it works differentiator unique

sections.title: Market Opportunity
sections.promptHint: Define TAM, SAM, and SOM with sources or methodology. Show a bottom-up logic for the SOM — how many customers × what ACV = what revenue. This should be achievable, not just aspirational.
sections.ragQuery: market size TAM SAM SOM addressable market opportunity

sections.title: Product
sections.promptHint: Describe the product in detail: core features, user flow, key capabilities. Write as if narrating a demo. Highlight what makes the UX or technical approach distinctive.
sections.ragQuery: product features capabilities user experience workflow demo

sections.title: Business Model
sections.promptHint: Explain how the company makes money: pricing model, contract structure, ARPU or ACV, gross margin if known. If available, include LTV/CAC ratio and payback period.
sections.ragQuery: business model pricing revenue monetization LTV CAC

sections.title: Traction
sections.promptHint: The most important slide. Include all available proof: ARR/MRR, growth rate, customer count, key logos, retention/churn, NPS. If pre-revenue, use pilots, LOIs, waitlist, or user growth. Mark missing data as [DATA NEEDED].
sections.ragQuery: traction revenue customers growth metrics ARR MRR retention churn

sections.title: Team
sections.promptHint: Explain WHY this specific team is uniquely positioned to win. Include: founder backgrounds, directly relevant experience, domain expertise, previous exits, key advisors. Focus on what makes them non-obvious for this problem.
sections.ragQuery: team founders experience background expertise advisors

sections.title: Financials
sections.promptHint: 3-year forward projections: revenue, headcount, burn rate, runway. List the key assumptions driving the model. Include current ARR and monthly burn. Use actuals from context if available.
sections.ragQuery: financials revenue projections burn rate runway headcount ARR

sections.title: The Ask
sections.promptHint: State: amount being raised, round type, how funds will be used (breakdown by category with %), and what milestone this funding takes the company to. End with the next 12–18 month roadmap at a high level.
sections.ragQuery: funding ask investment use of funds milestones roadmap next steps
